reasoning: Elon Musk was already TIME's Person of the Year in 2021. Historically, TIME magazine rarely awards the title to the same individual multiple times unless they are US Presidents winning a second term or highly pivotal geopolitical leaders. For corporate or tech figures, repeat selections are unprecedented. While Musk continues to command high media attention through X, Tesla, SpaceX, and the DOGE department in the incoming US administration, it is highly unlikely his 2025 activities will outshine major geopolitical leaders, broader cultural movements, or distinct novel events that TIME typically highlights. The prediction market consensus of ~3.7% aligns well with this being a longshot, though could be slightly overestimated due to his pervasive popularity.
